name: Product Inventory CSV Creation and Analysis
description: Create a CSV file with sample product data, read it back, calculate total inventory value, and save the result

tasks.name: Create CSV File
tasks.description: You MUST call file_write to create a CSV file named 'products.csv' with exactly 10 sample products. The CSV must have a header row: name,price,quantity. Then add 10 product rows with realistic data (e.g., 'Laptop,899.99,5'). Do NOT summarize - write the complete CSV content. Output format: Confirmation message including the full file path.
tasks.success_criteria: Output confirms CSV file was created and contains 10 product rows plus header

tasks.name: Read CSV File
tasks.description: You MUST call file_read to read the 'products.csv' file created in task_001. Output the file contents verbatim - do NOT summarize as 'file was read successfully'. Downstream tasks need the actual CSV data. Output format: The complete CSV content including all rows.
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains the complete CSV content with header row and 10 product rows

tasks.name: Calculate Inventory Value
tasks.description: You MUST call python_exec to calculate the total inventory value from task_002's CSV data. Write Python code that: 1) Parses the CSV content, 2) For each product row, calculates price * quantity, 3) Sums all values, 4) Prints the result. Use the csv module or simple string parsing. Output format: Print statement showing 'Total Inventory Value: $XXXX.XX'
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains a numeric total inventory value calculated from all 10 products

tasks.name: Save Result
tasks.description: You MUST call file_write to save the total inventory value from task_003 to a file named 'inventory_result.txt'. The file should contain a summary: the total value calculated and a brief note about the 10 products analyzed. Output format: Confirmation message with the file path.
tasks.success_criteria: Output confirms result file was created with the calculated inventory value
